title: The One Where Joey Dates Rachel

lines.text: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are entering carrying groceries and find Phoebe already there standing in front of a huge object that has been gift wrapped.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! I got you a present!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my goodness! Where did you hide it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I got it for your wedding and I ordered it weeks ago, and it finally got here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs you didn't have to get us anything for our wedding you already sang...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting) I love it! It's huge!! Let's open it! Open it!! (Monica rips open the paper.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a Ms. Pac-Man machine!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I didn't know where to put it so I just left it here for now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh well, maybe we can put it in the guest bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. (He goes to push it and it doesn't move.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I kinda like it here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you really like it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you kidding?! I practically spent my entire childhood at the arcade! This is like-Oh my, this is like my second favorite game!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh really? What was your first?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I-I really don't remember the name of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well what did it do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you just-you put a quarter in and y'know pull-pull some handles and win like a candy bar or something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A vending machine?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't feel bad for me! I won like every time!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Joey and Rachel are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Oh, I'm so glad you guys are here. I've been dying to tell someone what happened in the Paleontology department today.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Rachel) Do you think he saw us or can we still sneak out?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Professor Neuman, the head of the department, so....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They made you head of the department!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I get to teach one of his advanced classes! (Pause) Why didn't I get head of the department? (Goes and gets some coffee.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Hey Rach, listen umm...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got a big date coming up, do you know a good restaurant?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, Paul's Cafe. They got great food and it's really romantic.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, great! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Oh, and then afterwards you can take her to the Four Seasons for drinks. Or you go downtown and listen to some jazz. Or dancing-Oh! Take her dancing!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You sure are naming a lot of ways to postpone sex, I'll tell ya...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I miss dating. Gettin' all dressed up and going to a fancy restaurant. I'm not gonna be able to do that for so long, and it's so much fun! I mean not that sitting at home worrying about giving birth to a sixteen pound baby is not fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y'know what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why don't I take you out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Joey, you don't want to go on a date with a pregnant lady.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes I do! And we're gonna go out, we're gonna have a good time, and take your mind off of childbirth and c-sections and-and giant baby heads stretching out...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (interrupting) Okay! I'll go with ya! I'll go! I'll go with ya.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'll be fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right?
Monica and Chandler's, Monica is watching Phoebe play Ms. Pac-Man.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. No! Yes! Ahh. Oh, would you look at that Monica? I just knocked off all of your top scores, how sad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I'm next. (Phoebe starts another game.) Don't! Don't start another game! I said I'm next! Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. I didn't hear you over all the winning.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler! Phoebe's hogging the game!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who cares? It's a stupid game.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You only think it's stupid because you suck at it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't suck. It's sucks. You suck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait okay, if this game is gonna cause problems between the two of you, then maybe I should just keep it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No-no! I love it! It is a great present! In fact, why don't you go home and wait for the thank you card?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why do you want to play this game so bad?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! It's not like it spits out a Clark bar after every game.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Phoebe that's it. Come on, get out-out of the chair. Get out! (She goes to move Phoebe, but Phoebe goes limp and Monica can't move her.) Oh come...Phoebe!
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is getting ready for her date with Joey as there is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey? Could you get that? (There is no answer and she goes and opens the door to Joey.) What are you doing here? I thought you were in your room?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I'm picking you up for our date. These are for you. (Hands her some flowers.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, Lilies. Joey, they're my favorite. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And, a brownie! (Hands her a bag with the brownie in it.) Well, half a brownie. Actually, it's just bag. It's been a long walk from the flower shop and I was startin' to feel faint so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh man! This is so great! I actually feel like I'm going on a real date! Although, I have a hint of morning sickness, and I'm wearing underwear that goes up to about...(She snaps the waistband on her underwear that is just slightly below her breasts)...there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey come on now, this is a real date. Uh, so...nice place you got here. Foosball, huh? Pizza box. Oh, a subscription to Playboy, my kind of woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, actually that's my roommate's.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I would like to meet him. He sounds like a stand up guy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah yes, but he's very protective of me so you'd better watch yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah... Hey, so this roommate of yours...is he good looking?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, it must be tough to keep your hands of him, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Yeah, but I'm pretty sure he's gay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no, he's not! No! Why are you trying to ruin the game? Come on!
N.Y.U, Ross is teaching a class.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...which brings us back, of course, to Greely's theory of dominance. (The bell rings.) Okay, that-that's all for today. Oh, uh does anyone know where the Freeman building is?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yeah, it's the new building on Avenue A.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! That-that's all the way cross town, I'm supposed to teach a graduate seminar there in ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Ooh, dude. That's not gonna happen.
Ross grabs his things and runs to the door only to be blocked by his exiting students.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Move it! Move it! Move it!! Hey!!! I'm the teacher!!
The Freeman Building, Ross is entering his new class completely out of breath.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello. (He throws his jacket towards the coat rack and misses.) I'm sorry I'm a little late. Ah-(Checks his watch)-Whoa! A lot late. Let me start by uh, by introducing myself, I am Professor Geller. (The bell rings.) So to sum up, I'm Professor Geller. Good job today.
A Restaurant, a waitress is taking Joey and Rachel's dinner order.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now the filet mignon, what comes with that?

lines.speaker: Waitress
lines.text: There's a side of steamed vegetables.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Emmm. Now, instead of the vegetables, is there anyway I can substitute the three-pound lobster?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know what? Bring her both, and I'll have the same. (The waitress leaves.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! This is shaping up to be a pretty good date-Oh, I almost forgot. I didn't pay you the rent check.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh whoa-whoa-whoa, no roommate stuff. Okay? We're on a date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Wow! So I get to see what Joey Tribbiani is like on a date. So do you have any moves?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No. Umm, just myself and if they don't like me for-(Laughs.) I'm sorry I couldn't even get through that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I knew it! I knew it. Come on tell me your moves.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh alright. Umm, well, okay, I usually start by having a bottle of wine sent to my table from a fan.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. And that works?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well it does when you combine it with, "This is so embarrassing, I just want to have a normal life!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you poor little famous man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh okay, how about this one. I was gonna wait until the end of the night to kiss you, but you're so beautiful...I don't think I can.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looks interested) Oh my God! Wow! That was fantastic, I almost leaned in. I really almost did!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, so...so tell me one of your moves.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright. So where'd you grow up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's your move? Boy Rach, you're lucky you're hot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, just answer the question!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (exasperated) Queens.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And so were-were you close to your parents?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, with my mom. Yeah, not so much with my dad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. I guess there's just always been this distance y'know-I mean we both try to pretend it's not there, but it is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sympathetic) Oh. (Starts rubbing his wrist with her fingers.) It's gotta be rough.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it is. It's really tough. Y'know sometimes I think-Wow!! Nice move!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: "Where'd you grow up," it's so simple!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! And now if you'll excuse me, I have to go to the rest room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.
Rachel gets up and heads for the rest room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And now you're watching me walk away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes I am! Again so simple!
Monica and Chandler's, Monica is entering to find Chandler playing Ms. Pac-Man.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are not going to believe what I did today!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well it clearly wasn't showering or shaving.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got good. I played this game all day and now I rule at it! They should change the name of it to Ms. Chandler. (Pause) Although I-I hope they don't.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, you stayed home all day and played Ms. Pac-Man while I went off to work like some kind of chump?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-huh, and I got all the top ten scores, I erased Phoebe off the board! High five! (Holds up his hand to give Monica a high five, only he can't straighten his fingers.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is the matter with your hand?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I've been playing it for like eight hours, it'll loosen up. Come on, check out the scores. Oh, and also look at the initials, they're dirty words.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, why would you do that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because it's awesome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You think this is clever?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well y'know, they only give you three letters, so after A-S-S it is a bit of a challenge.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey wait a minute, this one isn't dirty.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It is when you put it together with that one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, well if you don't clear this off, you won't be getting one of those from me. But Ben's coming over here tomorrow to play this game, this can't be there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on, he won't even know what they mean.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler! He's seven; he's not stupid.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Have you talked to him lately?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, look I'm just gonna unplug it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no, if you unplug it, I'll have nothing to show for my day! It would be like I was at work. (She unplugs it.) No! (And plugs it back in.) Hey look at that! Look at that, it's still there! This thing must have some kind of primitive ROM (Read Only Memory, it's a memory chip.) Chip in it or something!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey you-you got to beat your scores!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: With the claw?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right fine. Fine, I'll do it. I've just got to get this off the screen. Carol and Susan are still upset that you taught him pull my finger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (smiles) Pull my finger-(Looks at his hand)-My hand is messed up.
Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Rachel are returning from their date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am not gonna answer that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on! Just pick one! Between Monica, Phoebe, Chandler, and Ross if you had to, if you had to, who would you punch?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No one! They are my friends, I wouldn't punch any of them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but I don't know why. Look at me, I'm having such a wonderful time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too! Hey Rach, can I just say I think this is the best date I ever had!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I never laughed so hard-Did you see the wine come out of my nose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I think everyone saw the wine come out of your nose.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I gotta say, I never knew I could enjoy the non-sex part of the date so much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that is because you have never been on a date with me before.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh. Huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, now don't judge me. I normally wait until my date leaves, but you live here. I'm ripping into this swan.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah! Okay, well then you don't judge me. I'm gonna suck on the cellophane from the brownie I had before. (They both do as they planned.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So tell me, what are Joey Tribbiani's end of the night moves?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, well, if I want the girl to kiss me, first thing I do is make my lips look irresistible.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How do you do that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now you can't tell anyone, but uh...I put on shiny lip balm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, like a moth to a flame, I'm telling ya. Okay all right, so now you go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I don't want to tell you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because it's embarrassing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: More embarrassing than shiny raspberry lip balm?! (Rachel just looks at him.) I didn't say raspberry before did I? All right just-just tell me Rach, just tell me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Okay. All right, stand up. (They do so.) Well, when we're at the door, I lightly press my lips against his, and then move into his body just for a second, and then I make this sound, "Hmmm." Okay, I know it doesn't sound like anything, but I swear it works.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stunned and turned on) Yeah. Why-Yeah, that would work for ya...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I gotta go to bed. Honey, I had such a wonderful time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. (She kisses him.) Yeah. (She goes into her room.) Me to. (He then starts to freak out.)
Commercial Break
Central Perk, Ross is telling Rachel about his class location predicament.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...you were 50 minutes late to the class, what did you crawl there?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I ran. It was really far, and when did people stop understanding the phrase, "Get the hell out of my way!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, why didn't you just take a cab?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, between the traffic that time of day and all the one-way streets it'll take me twice as long. Besides, I teach the class three times a week, who am I? Rockefeller?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well you're not gonna be able to keep doing this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I have to. Okay? If I don't, they'll take the class away from me. And...I already put it in my family newsletter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You've seen it, the Geller Yeller.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right. Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Besides, I-I think I figured out a much faster route, I'm sure I can make it this time. I just...I just can't be afraid to get a little bit...hit by cars. (He goes to the bathroom as Joey enters.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Hey, remember how last night we were talking about that movie Cujo?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, I still can't believe you haven't seen Cujo. What is wrong with you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Relax! It's not like it's Citizen Kane!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Have you ever tried to sit through Citizen Kane?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know it's really boring, but it's like a big deal. Anyway, I was thinking about renting Cujo sometime.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah? All right, let's do it tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well don't you have that big date tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: After our date last night, did you feel a little weird?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! You did too? It totally freaked me out, what was that?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know! (Pause) I'm-I'm kinda thinking it-it was the lobster...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah-yeah, the lobster.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I mean I was up sick all night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah me too, all night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! How come we didn't cross paths?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah well that's because uh...I stayed in my room. Yeah, you don't want to look in my hamper.
Monica and Chandler's, Monica is trying to erase Chandler's dirty words while he looks on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I got that. I'll escape over there. I'll come back over here. All right, come on Ms. Pac-Man. It's got-Right-(She dies.) Well, you're just a little bitch, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey, you guys. Listen, I'm sorry that I was hogging the game before-(Sees the top ten list)-Oh my God! Your friends have some unfortunate initials!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They're all Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler sucks! He couldn't have gotten this good!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did. (Looks at his still deformed hand) But it came at a price.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But Ben is coming over tonight and he can't see this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on, by age seven kids have already seen orgies. (They both look at him.) Was it just me?!
N.Y.U, Ross's new class, he is entering out of breath.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!!! Yes, I made it! I'm on time! (Grabs a bottle of water from a student, takes a drink, and splashes some on his face like the marathon runners do.) Okay, why don't we all uh, (Exhales loudly) open our books to page 23. Where... (Exhales again) Where you will see...a uh...a bunch of uh...red spots. Okay, (Closes his book.) umm, why don't, why don't you all start to read, while I-(Passes out and collapses.)
A Restaurant, Joey is on his hot date and they're not speaking right now.

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: Hey. Are you all right? You seem a little distracted.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! I'm fine. It's just... Hey, can I ask you something? Have you ever looked at someone that you've known for a while and then suddenly...suddenly see them a different way?

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: You mean like from behind?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, that...that's exactly it. You're right. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: Ew, y'know what? One time I saw this guy from behind and he seemed like a totally normal guy and then he turned around and it was Stephan Baldwin!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (deadpan) Ooh! Yeah. So yeah, so you know exactly what I'm talking about.

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: Totally! Wow! (Pause) Would you excuse me for a sec?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, sure. (She gets up, walks towards the bathroom, and Joey watches her go.) Ehhh.
Monica and Chandler's, now it's Phoebe's turn to erase Chandler from the board.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God Phoebe, you're on fire!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: One more score to go! You can do it! (Touches her shoulder.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't touch me!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't touch her!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! Go left! Go left! Go right!! Go right!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't!! I can't!! (She dies.) Noooooooo!!!!!!! You son of a bitch!!!!!

At this point a stream of obscenities burst forth from Phoebe's mouth just in time for Ben and Ross to enter and hear most of it, and in slow motion Ross tries to shield his innocent son from Phoebe's vulgarity.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hi Ben-No!! Don't look at the machine! (Covers the screen.)
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is watching Cujo while Joey returns from his date scaring Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God! Thank God you're home! I'm watching Cujo.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (incredulous) Alone?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! But what is wrong with this dog?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, did you get to the part where they're trapped in the car and Cujo's throwin' himself at the windshield?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! Seriously, what's wrong with the dog?! Wait a minute, what are you doing home so early? What happened to your date?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, it didn't work out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Do you want to watch the rest of the movie with me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, okay. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, I never thought I'd say this about a movie, but I really hope this dog dies. (Joey brings over a stool at sits on it next to Rachel who's in the big chair.) What are you doing over there? Come sit here, you protect me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh sure, yeah, why not? (Sits on the arm of the chair.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Pushes play.) Okay, that's him! That's him! That's Cujo! That's Cujo!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I know! I know. Yeah, it'll be okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God....What's he gonna do now? I can't watch! (Drags Joey closer to her and cowers into his chest.) Oh. Seriously, how can you watch this? Aren't you scared?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Terrified. (But for a totally different reason.)
Closing Credits
N.Y.U, Ross's new class, this time he's actually about to do a lecture.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So is everybody here? I got here a little early myself. Let us begin. Now, the hydrosaurids have been unearthed in two main locations. (He moves to the map and we see why he made it to class on time, he's wearing in-line skates and hasn't taken them off.) Here. (Points to the map, somewhere in the Middle East, then spins on the skates and points to the map.) Here. (China.) Now as for the hydrosaurs...

He tries to move in front of the class, only goes out of control and rolls into the hall, catching himself on the doorway with his pointer. He then pulls himself back into the room with the pointer, only he jams one end of it between the door frame and the door and breaks the pointer in half.
End
